activities.instructions: Select both conclusions that are grammatically correct and compatible with the evidence.
activities.prompt: The office is empty, the lights are off, and the alarm is set.

options.text: Everyone must have gone home.
Everyone must has gone home.
The last person can't have left.
The last person must have set the alarm.

activities.explanation: La oficina vacía apoya «must have gone» y la alarma activada implica que alguien debió configurarla.

activities.instructions: Select both conclusions that are grammatically correct and compatible with the evidence.
activities.prompt: Maya was on a live video call with us throughout the time the parcel disappeared.

options.text: Maya might have taken the parcel.
Maya can't have taken the parcel.
Someone else must have moved it.
Maya can't has taken it.

activities.explanation: La videollamada descarta a Maya y obliga a buscar otra persona como explicación.

activities.instructions: Select both conclusions that are grammatically correct and compatible with the evidence.
activities.prompt: The road is wet, but the weather report mentions both overnight rain and a broken water main.

options.text: It might have rained.
The water main could have caused it.
It must have snow yesterday.
The road can't have got wet.

activities.explanation: El contexto ofrece dos causas posibles; «might have rained» y «could have caused» reflejan esa incertidumbre.

activities.instructions: Select both conclusions that are grammatically correct and compatible with the evidence.
activities.prompt: The café is closed, although its website says it normally opens at eight.

options.text: The staff may have had an emergency.
The website might not have been updated.
The café must has opened normally.
The staff can't have faced any problem.

activities.explanation: Una emergencia y una web desactualizada son dos explicaciones posibles y correctamente formadas.

activities.instructions: Select both conclusions that are grammatically correct and compatible with the evidence.
activities.prompt: Leo's coat and phone are still on his desk, but he is not in the room.

options.text: He can't have gone far.
He may have stepped outside briefly.
He must have flown abroad.
He might has left permanently.

activities.explanation: Sus pertenencias hacen improbable que se haya ido lejos y permiten una salida breve.

activities.instructions: Select both conclusions that are grammatically correct and compatible with the evidence.
activities.prompt: The lock is undamaged and only three people have keys.

options.text: The door must have been forced.
Someone may have used a key.
One of the key holders could have entered.
Nobody can have access yesterday.

activities.explanation: La cerradura intacta hace posibles el uso de una llave y la entrada de una persona autorizada.

activities.instructions: Select both conclusions that are grammatically correct and compatible with the evidence.
activities.prompt: The train arrived empty after being delayed for four hours.

options.text: Most passengers must have found another route.
Some passengers may have cancelled their journeys.
Everyone can't have waited on board because it was empty.
Passengers must has stayed forever.

activities.explanation: Un tren vacío tras cuatro horas apoya una alternativa general y permite cancelaciones individuales.

activities.instructions: Select both conclusions that are grammatically correct and compatible with the evidence.
activities.prompt: The server restarted at 03.14, but no administrator was logged in.

options.text: An automated task must have triggered the restart.
A power fluctuation might have caused it.
An administrator must have clicked the button.
The server could restarted itself.

activities.explanation: Sin administrador, una tarea automática es una conclusión fuerte y un problema eléctrico sigue siendo posible.

activities.instructions: Select both conclusions that are grammatically correct and compatible with the evidence.
activities.prompt: The document contains terminology used only by the legal team.

options.text: A lawyer may have contributed to it.
It could have been based on a legal template.
The legal team can't have influenced it.
It must have wrote itself.

activities.explanation: La contribución de un abogado y una plantilla jurídica son explicaciones compatibles.

activities.instructions: Select both conclusions that are grammatically correct and compatible with the evidence.
activities.prompt: Aisha completed a six-hour task in twenty minutes, and a finished copy already existed.

options.text: She must have started from the existing copy.
Someone may have completed part of it earlier.
She can't have used any previous work.
She might have finish it from nothing.

activities.explanation: El tiempo y la copia previa apoyan reutilización o trabajo anterior de otra persona.

activities.instructions: Select both conclusions that are grammatically correct and compatible with the evidence.
activities.prompt: The package was signed for, but none of the residents recognises the signature.

options.text: A neighbour might have accepted it.
The courier could have signed in error.
A resident must have signed it knowingly.
The package can't have been delivered anywhere.

activities.explanation: Una recepción vecinal o un error del repartidor explican una firma desconocida.

activities.instructions: Select both conclusions that are grammatically correct and compatible with the evidence.
activities.prompt: The floor is covered in muddy footprints leading from the garden door.

options.text: Someone must have come in through the garden.
The person may have been wearing wet boots.
Nobody can have entered the room.
The footprints must have appeared tomorrow.

activities.explanation: La dirección de las huellas apoya la entrada por el jardín y permite inferir calzado mojado.

activities.instructions: Select both conclusions that are grammatically correct and compatible with the evidence.
activities.prompt: The sample was stored correctly, but the label is from a different batch.

options.text: The labels might have been switched.
Someone could have attached the wrong label.
The sample must have stored itself incorrectly.
The batch number can't have differed.

activities.explanation: Un intercambio de etiquetas o una etiqueta equivocada explican la discrepancia.

activities.instructions: Select both conclusions that are grammatically correct and compatible with the evidence.
activities.prompt: The archived file predates the software used to create it by five years.

options.text: The date can't have been recorded correctly.
The metadata must have been altered or corrupted.
The current software must have created it five years before existing.
The file may has travelled through time.

activities.explanation: La cronología imposible descarta la fecha literal y apunta a metadatos alterados o dañados.

activities.instructions: Select both conclusions that are grammatically correct and compatible with the evidence.
activities.prompt: Both witnesses use the same unusual phrase, although they were interviewed separately.

options.text: They may have heard the phrase from the same source.
One account could have influenced the other before the interviews.
Their wording can't have any common origin.
They must have inventing it independently.

activities.explanation: Una fuente común o una influencia previa son explicaciones posibles de una frase poco habitual.

activities.instructions: Select both conclusions that are grammatically correct and compatible with the evidence.
activities.prompt: The machine was unplugged, yet its internal clock shows the correct time.

options.text: It must have had a backup battery.
Its clock may have synchronised after reconnection.
It can't have had any source of power.
The clock could have keep time without energy.

activities.explanation: Una batería interna o una sincronización posterior explican el reloj correcto.

activities.instructions: Select both conclusions that are grammatically correct and compatible with the evidence.
activities.prompt: The application was submitted at midnight from an account whose owner was asleep.

options.text: The submission might have been scheduled.
Another authorised user could have submitted it.
The sleeping owner must have typed it consciously.
No one can have accessed the account.

activities.explanation: Una programación o un segundo usuario autorizado son compatibles con la ausencia del titular.

activities.instructions: Select both conclusions that are grammatically correct and compatible with the evidence.
activities.prompt: The plants are healthy despite nobody entering the locked office for a month.

options.text: An automatic system must have watered them.
They may have needed very little water.
An employee must have walked through the locked door.
They can't have received any water or light.

activities.explanation: El riego automático es una conclusión fuerte; una necesidad hídrica baja sigue siendo posible.

activities.instructions: Select both conclusions that are grammatically correct and compatible with the evidence.
activities.prompt: The figures in two independent reports match to the final decimal place, including one known error.

options.text: Both reports must have used the same underlying dataset.
One report may have copied data from the other.
The match can't have any shared cause.
Both teams must have made independently identical random errors.

activities.explanation: La coincidencia del error apoya una fuente compartida y permite la copia como explicación.

activities.instructions: Select both conclusions that are grammatically correct and compatible with the evidence.
activities.prompt: The sealed container weighs two kilograms less than its recorded dispatch weight.

options.text: Its contents may have been removed before sealing.
The dispatch weight could have been recorded incorrectly.
Two kilograms must have appeared inside it.
The weight can't have changed or been misrecorded.

activities.explanation: Una retirada previa o un error de registro explican la diferencia sin contradecir el sello.

activities.instructions: Select both conclusions that are grammatically correct and compatible with the evidence.
activities.prompt: The recording contains a voice in a room that was officially empty.

options.text: Someone might have entered without being logged.
The recording could have come from another time or source.
The room must have been both empty and occupied in the same sense.
The voice can't have been recorded anywhere.

activities.explanation: Una entrada no registrada o una atribución incorrecta de la grabación son posibilidades coherentes.

activities.instructions: Select both conclusions that are grammatically correct and compatible with the evidence.
activities.prompt: A supposedly original photograph contains a building completed ten years later.

options.text: The date must have been assigned incorrectly.
The image may have been edited later.
The building must have existed before construction.
The photograph can't have contained any later change.

activities.explanation: Una fecha incorrecta o una edición posterior explican el anacronismo.

activities.instructions: Select both conclusions that are grammatically correct and compatible with the evidence.
activities.prompt: The error appears only in records processed during one twenty-minute interval.

options.text: A temporary fault may have affected the system.
A configuration change could have been active briefly.
The permanent design must have caused every record ever created.
Nothing can have changed during that interval.

activities.explanation: La ventana limitada apunta a un fallo temporal o una configuración activa durante poco tiempo.

activities.instructions: Select both conclusions that are grammatically correct and compatible with the evidence.
activities.prompt: The witness describes details that were never released publicly.

options.text: The witness must have had access to non-public information.
Someone may have shared the details privately.
The witness can't have learned the details from any source.
The details must have become public without anyone knowing them.

activities.explanation: El conocimiento exige acceso no público, posiblemente mediante una comunicación privada.

activities.instructions: Select both conclusions that are grammatically correct and compatible with the evidence.
activities.prompt: The backup contains every file except those created during the power cut.

options.text: The backup process must have stopped during the cut.
Those files may not have been written to disk in time.
The missing files can't have any connection with the cut.
The backup must has included the missing files.

activities.explanation: La coincidencia temporal apoya una interrupción del respaldo y permite que los archivos no se guardaran.
